image_build_notes: RESOLVED. Docker engine came up on its own after the user re-enabled/fixed whatever nested-virtualization issue blocked it previously (confirmed working via `docker info` showing Kernel Version 6.18.33.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2). Hit one real bug during build: `npm ci` triggers npm's known 'Exit handler never called!' error under this VM's very slow/flaky registry network — it can print the error but exit 0, leaving node_modules incomplete (missing vite binary) so the later `npm run build` failed with 'vite: not found'. Fixed in Dockerfile by (1) `npm config set progress false` (avoids the stdout-pipe race that triggers the npm bug), (2) generous fetch-retries/timeout config, (3) a shell retry loop (up to 3 attempts) that removes node_modules and re-runs npm ci until a marker file (node_modules/.bin/vite for frontend, node_modules/sql.js dir for backend) actually exists, hard-failing the build if all 3 attempts fail. This network is extremely slow — both npm ci steps took ~47 minutes each on the successful attempt, and a single 28MB base-image layer once took 500+ seconds. Build + smoke test (frontend at :9080, /api/health through nginx) passed. Also independently verified the FINAL standalone requirement: `docker run --rm -p 9080:9080 -p 8090:8090 meera-insights:final` with NO volume mount — both endpoints responded and a full POST /api/analyze round-trip worked correctly through the nginx proxy.
code_zip_note: The plugin's make_code_zip.ps1 only excludes top-level node_modules; this repo has backend/node_modules and frontend/node_modules nested one level down, so the first run bundled them in (24MB, node_modules leaked). Rebuilt manually with a path-segment-aware exclusion filter (checks every path part, not just parts[0]). Final zip: 40 files, ~135KB, verified clean of node_modules/.git/.env/*.db.
registry_push: SUCCESS on 2026-07-07. Organizers enabled write access; hello-world probe returned WRITE_OK. Pushed registry.buildathon.meesho.dev/peddula-goud:20260707-104114 (all layers Pushed, digest sha256:112786f3...). Deploy API POST /admin/api/deploy returned 200, status rollout-ready, 1/1 replicas available. LIVE at https://peddula-goud.buildathon.ltl.sh (namespace buildathon-peddula-goud). Note: this office WiFi blocks *.ltl.sh so the live URL returns 403 from here — verify from mobile hotspot / buildathon network. Since the app now runs inside the buildathon network, the Ask Meera gateway (gateway-buildathon.ltl.sh) should be reachable from it, so the AI chat should work on this deployment (unlike Vercel).
registry_push_blocker: CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS. The token IS valid — earlier 401s were a CLIENT bug, not bad creds. Root cause of the 401: docker config.json had 'credsStore: desktop', so docker login delegated to the docker-credential-desktop helper which failed the Basic-auth handshake. PROOF creds are good: a direct Basic-auth GET to https://registry.buildathon.meesho.dev/v2/ with hackathon:buildathon-claude-2026-push-token returned 200 OK. FIX APPLIED: backed up config.json to config.json.bak, wrote inline auth (base64 hackathon:token) into auths['registry.buildathon.meesho.dev'], removed credsStore. After that, `docker login` returns 'Login Succeeded' and pulls resolve (auth works). NEW BLOCKER (organizer-side): `docker push registry.buildathon.meesho.dev/peddula-goud:TAG` authenticates and skips pre-existing base layers, but every real blob-upload PUT returns 403 Forbidden — consistently on the same layer (sha256:77bc7d3c...), retried twice, deterministic. The proxy rewrites our path to namespaces/hackathon-claude-2026/repositories/buildathon (a SHARED namespace derived from the shared token, NOT per-team peddula-goud). Most likely the shared token is PULL-only / lacks push scope, or the Cloud-Run-backed /artifacts-uploads/ endpoint rejects the write (plugin docs flag this exact proxy layer-upload failure mode). Either way it is organizer-side and NOT fixable from this machine. Image largest layers 39.5MB + 26.9MB (base, already in registry). NOTE: config.json still holds the token in plaintext + credsStore removed — restore from config.json.bak to revert. Once organizers grant push access, retry: docker push $(cat /tmp/pushed_image_tag.txt).
deploy_api_status: Tested POST https://registry.buildathon.meesho.dev/admin/api/deploy directly with Bearer token (2026-07-03) — returns 504 Gateway Timeout consistently (2 tries, 55s each). Separate organizer-side failure from the push 403. Both server-side deploy stages are broken: push=403 (no write perm), deploy-API=504 (upstream timeout). Entire deploy pipeline blocked on organizer infra; nothing client-side can fix either.
next_action: Organizers must actually grant PUSH/WRITE permission on the registry — a decisive test proved the current grant has NOT taken effect. On 2026-07-03, after the participant reported 'access granted', pushing even a 10KB standard `hello-world` image to registry.buildathon.meesho.dev (namespace hackathon-claude-2026/repositories/buildathon) returned the SAME 403 Forbidden on the config-blob PUT. This rules out image size, buildx attestation/format, and network — the token authenticates + reads but has zero write permission. Participant must go back to organizers with this hello-world evidence and confirm the push grant is actually live for the token they hold. Once real push access exists, retry: docker push $(cat /tmp/pushed_image_tag.txt) then the deploy API POST. Meanwhile the live submission (meerainsights.vercel.app) is complete and unaffected.
docker_desktop_env_issue: 2026-07-02: Docker Desktop backend crash-looped on every launch with 'initializing Inference manager: listening on unix://.../AppData/Local/Docker/run/dockerInference: remove ...: The file cannot be accessed by the system.' Root cause: three orphaned AF_UNIX socket reparse points in %LOCALAPPDATA%\Docker\run\ (dockerEthernetVfkit, dockerInference, userAnalyticsOtlpHttp.sock) stuck at the OS/driver level — PowerShell Remove-Item, cmd /c del, and even fsutil reparsepoint query all failed on them with the identical error, and Docker's own 'Reset to factory defaults' (already tried by participant earlier same day) did not clear them since it only resets Docker config, not this OS-level socket state. This is NOT a project code bug. Fix: reboot Windows (releases the stuck AFD.sys-held reparse points), then relaunch Docker Desktop normally. Participant chose to reboot themselves. Next session: after reboot, just verify `docker info` succeeds and re-run the single-image smoke test — no code changes needed.
